title: CNE Epic
description: Schema for epic JSON files consumed by the cne script (Create New Epic). An epic groups related atomic issues (CNIs) under a single strategic goal. Use this format exactly when creating new epics. Run: cne path/to/epic.json

title.description: Epic title. Format: [EPIC]: <concise name> — <subtitle>. Keep the name under 10 words; the subtitle gives directional intent.
examples: [EPIC]: Attention-First UIX — Incremental evolution toward distraction-free writing

label.description: GitHub label. Always 'epic' for this schema.

summary.description: One or two sentences capturing the strategic intent of the epic. Should answer: what are we evolving, and why?

problem_hl.description: High-level problem statement. Describe the user-facing or system-level pain in plain language. No solutions — focus on the gap, friction, or constraint.

problem_ll.description: Low-level problem domain. Use one of: '1' (system only), '2' (user only), '3' (both system and user). Indicates whether the epic addresses architecture, user experience, or both.

goals.description: Desired outcomes and design principles. Each item is a single, concrete goal or quality attribute the epic aims to deliver.

scope.description: Phased delivery plan. Each item describes one phase with its deliverables. Order implies priority. Format each item as 'Phase N: description'.

out_of_scope.description: Explicitly excluded work. Prevents scope creep. Each item names something that might be assumed in-scope but is deliberately deferred or omitted.

acceptance.description: Definition of Done for the epic as a whole. Testable, observable criteria. Each item must be verifiable — prefer measurable thresholds over subjective statements.

child_issues.description: Expected atomic issues (CNIs) that decompose this epic. Each item is a short identifier and description. Format: '<phase-prefix>-<seq> <short name>'. These become the basis for individual CNI files.

body.description: Full markdown body for the GitHub issue. Auto-generated from the structured fields above. Sections: Epic Summary, High-Level Problem Statement, Low-Level Problem Statement, Goals / Outcomes, In Scope, Out of Scope, Definition of Done, Expected Child Atomic Issues. If omitted, the cne script will render it from the other fields.
